summary: Solid 1986 show with standout Truckin', Fire On the Mountain and a rare Cold Rain & Snow, captured on a mostly soundboard matrix that sounds good despite some audience‑sourced tracks.
review: The performance is remembered as a highlight of the ’86 tour – fans rave about the driving Truckin' jam, the thunderous Fire on the Mountain (with an audible storm effect) and the only known Cold Rain & Snow → Fire sequence.  The recording is primarily a soundboard source mixed with a few audience‑only cuts, delivering generally good clarity though the patchwork nature makes it uneven in places.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the show and note decent sound quality, but a few call out mixed audio sources and give low star ratings, leading to an overall moderate rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Nice board sound noted by several reviewers; however four tracks are audience recordings causing occasional variance in clarity. Overall the mix is listenable and captures the ambience of the festival setting.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Opened as a warm‑up for Dylan & Petty, the second set built momentum with a thunderstorm effect during Fire; the concert is remembered for its energetic flow and rare song pairings.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir delivered spoken poetry during The Wheel.
